videos.title: Navio de guerra dos EUA 'COMPLETAMENTE DESTRUÍDO', oficiais da Marinha mortos? Irã revida após at...
videos.description:
A Guarda Revolucionária Islâmica do Irã (IRGC) afirmou ter atacado o porta-aviões USS Abraham Lincoln da Marinha dos EUA com mísseis de cruzeiro. Segundo a mídia iraniana, a IRGC disse que quatro mísseis Qader foram lançados contra o navio de guerra, que transporta caças avançados, incluindo F-35 e F/A-18. Teerã já havia alertado que os movimentos navais dos EUA na região estão sob constante vigilância e podem ser alvejados caso entrem no alcance dos mísseis. A alegação surge em meio a uma situação já volátil, com recentes ataques dos EUA supostamente atingindo infraestruturas importantes dentro do Irã, incluindo uma ponte em Karaj. Donald Trump também afirmou que os objetivos militares americanos no Irã estão próximos da conclusão, ao mesmo tempo em que alertou para novos ataques caso nenhum acordo seja alcançado.

#ussabrahamlincoln #irgc #iranmissiles #iranmissilestrike #irannavy #donaldtrump #iranwar2026

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Iran has claimed its missiles targeted US Navy's aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln. Meanwhile, the destroyers bring with them hundreds of missiles, which could include dozens of Tomahawk land attack cruise missiles. On March 25th, Iran's army had claimed that it targeted the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier with coastal cruise missiles. According to IRNA, the commander of the Iranian Navy, Rear Admiral Shaham Irani, had said that the movements of the US warship were under continuous monitoring and would be targeted once it entered the range of Iran's missile systems. Earlier, another US warship, the USS Gerald R. Ford, which had been deployed in the Middle East, caught fire last month. The mishap injured sailors and destroyed 100 beds. According to the Guardian, an official said nearly 200 sailors were treated for smoke related injuries when the fire broke out in the warship's main laundry area. Donald Trump had ordered the USS Gerald R. forward into the Middle East from the Caribbean last month to join other warships and military assets the US had built up in the region ahead of the start of the conflict in the Middle East. Earlier, US strikes destroyed the B1 bridge, which was still under construction, killing at least eight people and wounded 95. In a truth social post, Trump shared the video footage of the strike and wrote, "The biggest bridge in Iran comes tumbling down, never to be used again, much more to follow. It is time for Iran to make a deal before it is too late, and there is nothing left of what still could become a great country." Donald Trump has already said that the US military core strategic objectives in Iran were nearing completion. We totally obliterated those nuclear sites. The regime then sought to rebuild their nuclear program at a totally different location, making clear they had no intention of abandoning their pursuit of nuclear weapons. They were also rapidly building a vast stockpile of conventional ballistic missiles and would soon have had missiles that could reach the American homeland, Europe, and virtually any other place on Earth. We just learned that out. We took them out. We took them all out. So that no one would really dare stop them. And their race for a nuclear bomb, a nuclear weapon, a nuclear weapon like nobody's ever seen before. They were right at the doorstep. We are systematically dismantling the regime's ability to threaten America or project power outside of their borders. As we celebrate this progress, we think especially of the 13 American warriors who have laid down their lives in this fight to prevent our children from ever having to face a nuclear Iran. Thanks to the progress we've made, I can say tonight that we are on track to complete all of America's military objectives shortly. If there is no deal, we are going to hit each and every one of their electric generating plants very hard and probably simultaneously. We have not hit their oil, even though that's the easiest target of all because it would not give them even a small chance of survival or rebuilding. But we could hit it and it would be gone and there's not a thing they could do about it. They have no anti-aircraft equipment. Their radar is 100% annihilated. We are unstoppable as a military force. The nuclear sites that we obliterated with the B2 bombers have been hit so hard that it would take months to get near the nuclear dust and we have it under intense satellite surveillance and control. If we see them make a move, even a move for it will hit them with missiles very hard. Again, we have all the cards. They have none. It's very important that we keep this conflict in perspective. videos.title: 'PRIMEIRO ATAQUE COM MÍSSEIS EM AL-ARQAB': Ataque mortal contra base americana, mísseis de cruzei...
videos.description:
A Resistência Islâmica do Iraque reivindicou seis ataques coordenados contra bases militares americanas em 24 horas, marcando uma escalada significativa. Explosões foram relatadas em Erbil após ataques com drones contra posições americanas. Um ataque separado também atingiu uma base de apoio americana perto do Aeroporto Internacional de Bagdá. Embora o número de vítimas ainda não tenha sido confirmado, os ataques sinalizam uma crescente pressão sobre as forças americanas. O grupo afirma que as operações continuarão até que as tropas estrangeiras deixem o Iraque. Com as tensões aumentando em toda a região, esses acontecimentos podem desencadear uma instabilidade ainda maior. Assista a esta reportagem para as últimas atualizações e análises aprofundadas.

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição A fresh wave of coordinated strikes targeted US positions in Iraq. The Islamic resistance of Iraq has claimed responsibility for six precise operations in just 24 hours. A bold escalation aimed directly at American military presence in the region. According to a statement from Sarah Alia aldam, these strikes are part of an ongoing campaign to force the withdrawal of what it calls occupation forces. The attacks appear to be widespread and coordinated. Multiple explosions were reported in air bill, a key hub in the Iraqi Kurdistan region. Local sources say several kamicazi drones were launched at US targets. The drones reportedly struck their intended objectives with precision. At the same time, another attack was reported near Baghdad International Airport. A US military support base in the area was hit by a drone strike. Footage also shows the Islamic resistance in Iraq launching an Alarab missile toward US forces bases, signaling heightened regional tensions. An Iraqi official confirmed the incident but did not provide further details. There is still no official word on casualties. The resistance group has remained consistent in its messaging. It says operations will continue until all foreign troops leave Iraqi soil. This aligns with the broader agenda of what is often described as the axis of resistance, a network of groups opposing US influence across the Middle East. The intensity of these attacks raises serious questions. Is this a new phase of sustained pressure on US forces or the beginning of a wider regional escalation? Washington has yet to respond in detail, but the pattern is clear. Drone warfare is becoming the weapon of choice. Fast, precise, and difficult to counter. As tensions rise across the region, Iraq is once again at the center of a dangerous geopolitical storm. A massive and coordinated assault is shaking the Middle East tonight. Iran has unleashed one of its most intense missile barges yet. And this time, the battlefield stretches far beyond Israel alone. The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps has confirmed the launch of wave 91. This is part of Operation True Promise 4, a rapidly escalating campaign. Heavy ballistic missiles and attack drones are now striking key targets. The primary focus is Israel's major urban centers. Tel Aviv and Hifer are at the heart of this offensive. According to Iranian claims, the impact has been immediate and severe. 5 million people have reportedly rushed into underground bunkers. Air raid sirens have echoed across cities as explosions intensify. The scale of panic reflects the magnitude of the incoming firepower. The situation on the ground is described as chaotic and overwhelming. Emergency responders are struggling to keep pace with the strikes. Successive explosions have created confusion across multiple locations. But this is not a single front war anymore. Iran has made it clear this is a coordinated regional offensive. New fronts have opened, expanding the scope of the conflict dramatically. From the south, Yemen has entered the battlefield. Onsar forces have launched attacks towards southern Israeli territories. Targets reportedly include sensitive areas near Deona. At the same time, Hezbollah has joined the operation. A joint strike was carried out in Tel Aviv's Jaffa region. This marks a rare and dangerous level of coordination. Iran, Hezbollah, and Yemen are now acting in unison. Officials describe it as a unified resistance front. This alignment signals a long prepared and synchronized strategy. According to Israeli broadcaster Khn, the damage is substantial. A powerful Iranian missile with a heavy warhead struck key locations. Several sites have suffered significant destruction. Strategic Israeli air bases have also come under heavy attack. Teln Paul Mahim and Bengurian airport were reportedly struck. These are among Israel's most critical military installations. Urban centers have not been spared either. Tel Aviv, Hifer, AOT, Bashiba, and the Negev region are affected. Missiles and drones have caused widespread damage and casualties. The conflict is no longer confined to Israeli territory. US military assets across the region have also been targeted. This marks a significant widening of the confrontation. Iran claims it fired four gutter missiles at a US aircraft carrier. The USS Abraham Lincoln was reportedly targeted in this wave. There is no independent confirmation of the extent of damage. US bases in Uwait have also been hit. Ahmed al- Jabar and Ali al-Salm bases reportedly took direct strikes. Both installations are considered strategically vital. In Saudi Arabia, the Alhaj base was also targeted. Iran claims precision ballistic missiles caused heavy damage there. This signals a direct challenge to US military presence. The UAE has also been drawn into the conflict. A reported strike hit a gathering of US flight engineers and pilots. The attack allegedly took place near a major American base. Even more significant is the reported destruction of a radar system. The early warning system at Aldafra base was completely wiped out. This system played a key role in detecting aerial threats. Its loss could impact regional defense capabilities and it raises serious concerns about vulnerability in the Gulf. Iran has also made a dramatic claim in the Persian Gulf. It says an advanced Israeli fighter jet was shot down. According to the IRGC, the jet was hit by naval air defenses. It crashed into waters between Hangam and Keshum Islands. There is no independent verification of this claim so far. Meanwhile, the IRGC claimed that dozens of Americans were either killed or injured in its Wave 90 strikes.

videos.title: 'MILHÕES EM BUNKERS': Irã lança ataque massivo contra Israel; Houthis e Hezbollah se juntam ao at...
videos.description:
A Guarda Revolucionária Islâmica do Irã (IRGC) lançou a Onda 91 da Operação Verdadeira Promessa 4, disparando mísseis pesados ​​e drones contra cidades israelenses importantes, forçando milhões de pessoas a buscar abrigo em meio a pânico e destruição generalizados. O Hezbollah e o Ansarullah do Iêmen juntaram-se ao ataque coordenado, visando locais estratégicos, incluindo bases aéreas e infraestrutura crítica. Bases militares americanas no Kuwait e na Arábia Saudita também foram atingidas, sinalizando um conflito crescente. O Irã alega ainda ter atacado o porta-aviões USS Abraham Lincoln e destruído um importante sistema de radar dos Emirados Árabes Unidos, intensificando os temores de uma guerra regional em rápida expansão com consequências globais.

videos.title: Irã, Houthis e Hezbollah lançam um ataque conjunto devastador contra Israel; mísseis atingem as F...
videos.description:
Sirenes de ataque aéreo, mísseis no céu e ameaças de múltiplas frentes. Israel está novamente sob ataque, desta vez do Iêmen, Irã e Líbano, quase simultaneamente. Os militares israelenses afirmam ter interceptado um míssil balístico lançado pelas forças Houthi, apoiadas pelo Irã, no Iêmen, em 2 de abril, impedindo o que poderia ter sido um grande ataque. Sirenes soaram por todo Israel, áreas vizinhas e regiões próximas ao Mar Morto, fazendo com que civis corressem para abrigos. Em um desenvolvimento separado, as Forças de Defesa de Israel confirmaram outro ataque com míssil balístico, desta vez diretamente do Irã, visando o centro de Israel.

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Air raid sirens, missiles in the sky, and threats from multiple fronts. Israel is once again under fire, this time from Yemen, Iran, and Lebanon almost simultaneously. The Israeli military says it intercepted a ballistic missile launched by Iranbacked Houthi forces in Yemen on April 2, preventing what could have been a major strike. Sirens blared across Israel, surrounding areas, and regions near the Dead Sea, sending civilians rushing into shelters. Despite the panic, no injuries have been reported so far. But the threat didn't stop there. In a separate development, the Israeli Defense Forces confirmed another ballistic missile attack, this time directly from Iran, targeting central Israel. Warning sirens echoed across multiple communities as air defenses were activated once again. Additional operations by the 146th and 162nd divisions reportedly led to the destruction of weapons and infrastructure in the area. With ground operations ongoing and crossber strikes continuing, the northern front remains volatile with the risk of further escalation. According to the Israeli military around 48 soldiers were wounded over a 24-hour period with additional reports of fatalities including among personnel from the Nahal brigade. Earlier, the military had said that at least 10 officers and soldiers have been killed since the start of the current conflict involving Iran and Lebanon, while over 300 have been injured, including several in serious condition. Israeli assessments indicate that the fighting remains intense and complex, particularly in ground operations. Military sources have pointed to the challenges posed by Hezbollah's tactics, including rocket fire, ambushes, and mobility across terrain, which have contributed to ongoing casualties and operational pressure. At the same time, Hezbollah has continued to carry out attacks targeting Israeli military positions along the border as part of the broader confrontation. In one of the recent incidents in southern Lebanon, Israeli media reported that four soldiers, including an officer, were killed while three others were injured, one of them seriously. According to the Israel Defense Forces, one soldier was moderately injured after being hit by shrapnel when a rocket struck near Israeli troops. In a separate incident, three reserveists sustained light injuries after a drone exploded in close proximity to their position. All four soldiers were evacuated for medical treatment with the IDF stating that their families have been notified. The incidents reflect the continued use of mixed attack tactics by Hezbollah, combining rocket fire with drone strikes to target Israeli forces near the border. While the injuries were limited, the back-to back nature of the incidents highlights the persistent volatility of the Israel Lebanon front amid the wider regional conflict. As tensions remain high, the northern front continues to act as a secondary but active theater, adding to the multiffront pressure on Israeli forces. The deceased soldiers were identified as Captain Noah Madmoni, Sergeant Ben Cohen, Sergeant Liron Benzion, and Sergeant Maxim Antis. The IDF also said the Israeli Navy targeted a Hezbollah weapons depot in southern Lebanon, highlighting coordinated operations across multiple domains. On the ground, Israeli divisions continued to operate in southern Lebanon. The military said troops from the 91st Galilee Division identified and struck a Hezbollah cell while the 36th Division directed a strike on armed operatives on motorcycles, killing several fighters. Additional operations by the 146th and 162nd divisions reportedly led to the destruction of weapons and infrastructure in the area. With ground operations ongoing and crossber strikes continuing, the northern front remains volatile with the risk of further escalation. Israeli authorities have reported rising casualties amid ongoing fighting with Hezbollah in southern Lebanon as crossber hostilities continue alongside the wider regional escalation. According to the Israeli military around 48 soldiers were wounded over a 24-hour period with additional reports of fatalities including among personnel from the Nahal brigade. Earlier, the military had said that at least 10 officers and soldiers have been killed since the start of the current conflict involving Iran and Lebanon, while over 300 have been injured, including several in serious condition. Israeli assessments indicate that the fighting remains intense and complex, particularly in ground operations. Military sources have pointed to the challenges posed by Hezbollah's tactics, including rocket fire, ambushes, and mobility across terrain, which have contributed to ongoing casualties and operational pressure. At the same time, Hezbollah has continued to carry out attacks targeting Israeli military positions along the border as part of the broader confrontation. In one of the recent incidents in southern Lebanon, Israeli media reported that four soldiers, including an officer, were killed while three others were injured, one of them seriously. According to the Israel Defense Forces, one soldier was moderately injured after being hit by shrapnel when a rocket struck near Israeli troops. In a separate incident, three reserveists sustained light injuries after a drone exploded in close proximity to their position. All four soldiers were evacuated for medical treatment with the IDF stating that their families have been notified. The incidents reflect the continued use of mixed attack tactics by Hezbollah, combining rocket fire with drone strikes to target Israeli forces near the border. While the injuries were limited, the back-to back nature of the incidents highlights the persistent volatility of the Israel Lebanon front amid the wider regional conflict. A Guarda Revolucionária Islâmica do Irã (IRGC) lançou a Onda 91 da Operação Verdadeira Promessa 4, disparando mísseis pesados ​​e drones contra cidades israelenses importantes, forçando milhões de pessoas a buscar abrigo em meio a pânico e destruição generalizados. O Hezbollah e o Ansarullah do Iêmen juntaram-se ao ataque coordenado, visando locais estratégicos, incluindo bases aéreas e infraestrutura crítica. Bases militares americanas no Kuwait e na Arábia Saudita também foram atingidas, sinalizando um conflito crescente. O Irã alega ainda ter atacado o porta-aviões USS Abraham Lincoln e destruído um importante sistema de radar dos Emirados Árabes Unidos, intensificando os temores de uma guerra regional em rápida expansão com consequências globais.

videos.title: 'PRIMEIRO ATAQUE COM MÍSSEIS EM AL-ARQAB': Ataque mortal contra base americana, mísseis de cruzei...
videos.description:
A Resistência Islâmica do Iraque reivindicou seis ataques coordenados contra bases militares americanas em 24 horas, marcando uma escalada significativa. Explosões foram relatadas em Erbil após ataques com drones contra posições americanas. Um ataque separado também atingiu uma base de apoio americana perto do Aeroporto Internacional de Bagdá. Embora o número de vítimas ainda não tenha sido confirmado, os ataques sinalizam uma crescente pressão sobre as forças americanas. O grupo afirma que as operações continuarão até que as tropas estrangeiras deixem o Iraque. Com as tensões aumentando em toda a região, esses acontecimentos podem desencadear uma instabilidade ainda maior. Assista a esta reportagem para as últimas atualizações e análises aprofundadas.

videos.title: Irã, Houthis e Hezbollah lançam um ataque conjunto devastador contra Israel; mísseis atingem as F...
videos.description:
Sirenes de ataque aéreo, mísseis no céu e ameaças de múltiplas frentes. Israel está novamente sob ataque, desta vez do Iêmen, Irã e Líbano, quase simultaneamente. Os militares israelenses afirmam ter interceptado um míssil balístico lançado pelas forças Houthi, apoiadas pelo Irã, no Iêmen, em 2 de abril, impedindo o que poderia ter sido um grande ataque. Sirenes soaram por todo Israel, áreas vizinhas e regiões próximas ao Mar Morto, fazendo com que civis corressem para abrigos. Em um desenvolvimento separado, as Forças de Defesa de Israel confirmaram outro ataque com míssil balístico, desta vez diretamente do Irã, visando o centro de Israel.

#Irã #Israel #Houthi #GuerraIrãIsrael #Hezbollah

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Air raid sirens, missiles in the sky, and threats from multiple fronts. Israel is once again under fire, this time from Yemen, Iran, and Lebanon almost simultaneously. The Israeli military says it intercepted a ballistic missile launched by Iranbacked Houthi forces in Yemen on April 2, preventing what could have been a major strike. Sirens blared across Israel, surrounding areas, and regions near the Dead Sea, sending civilians rushing into shelters. Despite the panic, no injuries have been reported so far. But the threat didn't stop there. In a separate development, the Israeli Defense Forces confirmed another ballistic missile attack, this time directly from Iran, targeting central Israel. Warning sirens echoed across multiple communities as air defenses were activated once again. At the same time, a drone infiltration from Lebanon triggered fresh alerts in the Western Galilee, signaling yet another front opening up in this widening conflict. This coordinated wave of threats highlights a dangerous escalation with Israel now facing simultaneous attacks from multiple Iran aligned groups across the region. While Israel claims its defenses are holding, the growing complexity of these attacks is raising serious concerns about what comes next. Meanwhile, a spokesperson for Israel's military said the army had anticipated attempts by Iran and Hezbollah to quote unquote disrupt the Passover holiday. Yesterday, we marked the seder night across the country. Ahead of the holiday, we prepared that Iran and Hezbollah would try to continue the firing toward Israel and to disturb the eve of the holiday. The air defense systems of the IDF, Israel Defense Forces, intercepted most of the launches. As part of our effort to thwart and disrupt the firing, beginning yesterday in the afternoon hours, the Air Force struck dozens of targets of missile arrays in Iran and Lebanon. In Iran, we continue to deepen and complete the damage to the regime's military production industries. This is a terror industry that empowers the forces of the regime. Hezbollah launched more than 50 rockets from Lebanon toward northern Israel on the first day of Passover, according to Israeli assessments, forcing hundreds of thousands of civilians into shelters as sirens sounded across the region. One of the rockets struck the border city of Kirat Scha where two people were lightly injured. Emergency services said the barrage marked another escalation in crossber fire with attacks continuing despite heightened civilian movement during the holiday. In a separate attack, a barrage of around five rockets was launched toward the Kryat suburbs near Hifa. The Israel Defense Forces said most of the rockets were intercepted while others were allowed to fall in open areas according to protocol. No injuries were reported in that incident. Meanwhile, the Israeli Defense Forces said it carried out a series of strikes across southern Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah positions over the past 24 hours. According to the military, more than 40 Hezbollah operatives were killed, and dozens of sites linked to the group were hit. The Israeli Air Force reportedly struck command centers, weapons depots, rocket launchers, and anti-tank missile positions in multiple locations. The IDF also said the Israeli Navy targeted a Hezbollah weapons depot in southern Lebanon, highlighting coordinated operations across multiple domains. On the ground, Israeli divisions continued to operate in southern Lebanon. The military said troops from the 91st Galilee Division identified and struck a Hezbollah cell while the 36th Division directed a strike on armed operatives on motorcycles, killing several fighters. Additional operations by the 146th and 162nd divisions reportedly led to the destruction of weapons and infrastructure in the area. While Hezbollah maintains pressure through rocket barges targeting northern Israel, Israeli forces appear focused on degrading the group's infrastructure and operational capabilities inside southern Lebanon. With ground operations ongoing and crossber strikes continuing, the northern front remains volatile with the risk of further escalation. Israeli authorities have reported rising casualties amid ongoing fighting with Hezbollah in southern Lebanon as crossber hostilities continue alongside the wider regional escalation. According to the Israeli military around 48 soldiers were wounded over a 24-hour period with additional reports of fatalities including among personnel from the Nahal brigade. Earlier, the military had said that at least 10 officers and soldiers have been killed since the start of the current conflict involving Iran and Lebanon, while over 300 have been injured, including several in serious condition. Separately, Israel's health ministry said 118 injured individuals were admitted to hospitals on Tuesday, bringing the total number of people treated in Israeli hospitals since February 28th to 6,638 cases. Israeli assessments indicate that the fighting remains intense and complex, particularly in ground operations. Military sources have pointed to the challenges posed by Hezbollah's tactics, including rocket fire, ambushes, and mobility across terrain, which have contributed to ongoing casualties and operational pressure. At the same time, Hezbollah has continued to carry out attacks targeting Israeli military positions along the border as part of the broader confrontation. In one of the recent incidents in southern Lebanon, Israeli media reported that four soldiers, including an officer, were killed while three others were injured, one of them seriously. Four Israeli soldiers were wounded in separate Hezbollah attacks in southern Lebanon, the Israeli military said, as crossber hostilities along the northern front continue. According to the Israel Defense Forces, one soldier was moderately injured after being hit by shrapnel when a rocket struck near Israeli troops. In a separate incident, three reserveists sustained light injuries after a drone exploded in close proximity to their position. All four soldiers were evacuated for medical treatment with the IDF stating that their families have been notified. The incidents reflect the continued use of mixed attack tactics by Hezbollah, combining rocket fire with drone strikes to target Israeli forces near the border. While the injuries were limited, the back-to back nature of the incidents highlights the persistent volatility of the Israel Lebanon front amid the wider regional conflict. As tensions remain high, the northern front continues to act as a secondary but active theater, adding to the multiffront pressure on Israeli forces. The Israeli Defense Forces said four soldiers were killed during fighting with Iranbacked Hezbollah fighters in southern Lebanon. The deceased soldiers were identified as Captain Noah Madmoni, Sergeant Ben Cohen, Sergeant Liron Benzion, and Sergeant Maxim Antis. The funeral of Sergeant Lan Benzion, killed by an anti-tank missile fired by Hezbollah, was held in the central town of Holan. The deaths bring the total number of troops killed in the war to 10. May your soul in heaven watch over all of Israel. May we finally receive good news. Enough already. Enough of these funerals. Enough of this pain. Children lying here in the ground. Emergency services said the barrage marked another escalation in crossber fire with attacks continuing despite heightened civilian movement during the holiday. In a separate attack, a barrage of around five rockets was launched toward the Kryat suburbs near Hifa. The Israel Defense Forces said most of the rockets were intercepted while others were allowed to fall in open areas according to protocol. No injuries were reported in that incident. Meanwhile, the Israeli Defense Forces said it carried out a series of strikes across southern Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah positions over the past 24 hours. According to the military, more than 40 Hezbollah operatives were killed, and dozens of sites linked to the group were hit. The Israeli Air Force reportedly struck command centers, weapons depots, rocket launchers, and anti-tank missile positions in multiple locations. The IDF also said the Israeli Navy targeted a Hezbollah weapons depot in southern Lebanon, highlighting coordinated operations across multiple domains. On the ground, Israeli divisions continued to operate in southern Lebanon. The military said troops from the 91st Galilee Division identified and struck a Hezbollah cell while the 36th Division directed a strike on armed operatives on motorcycles, killing several fighters. Additional operations by the 146th and 162nd divisions reportedly led to the destruction of weapons and infrastructure in the area. While Hezbollah maintains pressure through rocket barges targeting northern Israel, Israeli forces appear focused on degrading the group's infrastructure and operational capabilities inside southern Lebanon. With ground operations ongoing and crossber strikes continuing, the northern front remains volatile with the risk of further escalation. Israeli authorities have reported rising casualties amid ongoing fighting with Hezbollah in southern Lebanon as crossber hostilities continue alongside the wider regional escalation. According to the Israeli military around 48 soldiers were wounded over a 24-hour period with additional reports of fatalities including among personnel from the Nahal brigade. Earlier, the military had said that at least 10 officers and soldiers have been killed since the start of the current conflict involving Iran and Lebanon, while over 300 have been injured, including several in serious condition. Separately, Israel's health ministry said 118 injured individuals were admitted to hospitals on Tuesday, bringing the total number of people treated in Israeli hospitals since February 28th to 6,638 cases. Israeli assessments indicate that the fighting remains intense and complex, particularly in ground operations. Military sources have pointed to the challenges posed by Hezbollah's tactics, including rocket fire, ambushes, and mobility across terrain, which have contributed to ongoing casualties and operational pressure. At the same time, Hezbollah has continued to carry out attacks targeting Israeli military positions along the border as part of the broader confrontation. In one of the recent incidents in southern Lebanon, Israeli media reported that four soldiers, including an officer, were killed while three others were injured, one of them seriously. Four Israeli soldiers were wounded in separate Hezbollah attacks in southern Lebanon, the Israeli military said, as crossber hostilities along the northern front continue. According to the Israel Defense Forces, one soldier was moderately injured after being hit by shrapnel when a rocket struck near Israeli troops. In a separate incident, three reserveists sustained light injuries after a drone exploded in close proximity to their position. All four soldiers were evacuated for medical treatment with the IDF stating that their families have been notified. The incidents reflect the continued use of mixed attack tactics by Hezbollah, combining rocket fire with drone strikes to target Israeli forces near the border. While the injuries were limited, the back-to back nature of the incidents highlights the persistent volatility of the Israel Lebanon front amid the wider regional conflict. As tensions remain high, the northern front continues to act as a secondary but active theater, adding to the multiffront pressure on Israeli forces. The Israeli Defense Forces said four soldiers were killed during fighting with Iranbacked Hezbollah fighters in southern Lebanon. The deceased soldiers were identified as Captain Noah Madmoni, Sergeant Ben Cohen, Sergeant Liron Benzion, and Sergeant Maxim Antis. A Guarda Revolucionária Islâmica do Irã (IRGC) lançou a Onda 91 da Operação Verdadeira Promessa 4, disparando mísseis pesados ​​e drones contra cidades israelenses importantes, forçando milhões de pessoas a buscar abrigo em meio a pânico e destruição generalizados. O Hezbollah e o Ansarullah do Iêmen juntaram-se ao ataque coordenado, visando locais estratégicos, incluindo bases aéreas e infraestrutura crítica. Bases militares americanas no Kuwait e na Arábia Saudita também foram atingidas, sinalizando um conflito crescente. O Irã alega ainda ter atacado o porta-aviões USS Abraham Lincoln e destruído um importante sistema de radar dos Emirados Árabes Unidos, intensificando os temores de uma guerra regional em rápida expansão com consequências globais.
